title: [BUG] nfs volume per quadlet not working
body:
### Issue Description

I have the problem, that when i want to create a nfs based volume with podman quadlet in an rootless environment than the nfs options are not getting used by creating the volume.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. the whole rootless setup
2. created a .volume file in ~/.config/containers/systemd/ with the nfs config
3 .systemctl --user daemon-reload


### Describe the results you received

This volume is getting created:
[n8n@privatepodman systemd]$ podman volume inspect nfs_n8n
```
[
     {
          "Name": "nfs_n8n",
          "Driver": "local",
          "Mountpoint": "/srv/n8n/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/nfs_n8n/_data",
          "CreatedAt": "2025-03-21T23:15:39.227804604Z",
          "Labels": {},
          "Scope": "local",
          "Options": {},
          "UID": 1000,
          "GID": 1000,
          "MountCount": 0,
          "NeedsCopyUp": true,
          "NeedsChown": true,
          "LockNumber": 1
     }
]
```


### Describe the results you expected

i would eexpect this volume:
```
[n8n@privatepodman systemd]$ podman volume create --driver local   --opt type=nfs4   --opt o=addr=192.168.20.2,rw,async,hard,intr,nfsvers=4.2   --opt device=:/n8n   n8n-test
n8n-test
[n8n@privatepodman systemd]$ podman volume inspect n8n-test
[
     {
          "Name": "n8n-test",
          "Driver": "local",
          "Mountpoint": "/srv/n8n/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/n8n-test/_data",
          "CreatedAt": "2025-03-21T23:27:10.5149836Z",
          "Labels": {},
          "Scope": "local",
          "Options": {
               "device": ":/n8n",
               "o": "addr=192.168.20.2,rw,async,hard,intr,nfsvers=4.2",
               "type": "nfs4"
          },
          "MountCount": 0,
          "NeedsCopyUp": true,
          "NeedsChown": true,
          "LockNumber": 2
     }
]

```

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Its an rocky linux system in the newest version

### Additional information

I first tried it to deploy it with ansible and that didnt worked with the following script:
```
- name: Deploy n8n using Podman Quadlet in user mode
  hosts: privatepodman
  become: true
  gather_facts: false
  vars:
    target_user: n8n
  tasks:
    - name: Create nfs_n8n.volume (NFS Volume)
      become_user: "{{ target_user }}"
      containers.podman.podman_volume:
        name: NFS volume for n8n
        state: quadlet
        quadlet_dir: /srv/"{{ target_user }}"/.config/containers/systemd/
        quadlet_file_mode: "0600"
        quadlet_filename: nfs_n8n.volume
        driver: local
        options:
          - type=nfs4
          - o=addr=192.168.20.2,rw,async,hard,intr,nfsvers=4.2
          - device=:/n8n
```

after some different tries with ansible I tried it than directly with editing the .volume file for the user and that also didnt worked.
Now after im trying this now for ~3h with different versions i also tried it with podlet to let me create the file and even this one is not working as proof:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c2308ffa-c95c-4a6f-ac73-89d5e6532ece)

```
[n8n@privatepodman ~]$ cd podlet-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
[n8n@privatepodman podlet-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu]$ ./podlet podman volume create --driver local   --opt type=nfs4   --opt o=addr=192.168.20.2,rw,async,hard,intr,nfsvers=4.2   --opt device=:/n8n   n8n-vol
# n8n-vol.volume
[Volume]
Device=:/n8n
Driver=local
Options=addr=192.168.20.2,rw,async,hard,intr,nfsvers=4.2
Type=nfs4
[n8n@privatepodman podlet-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu]$ cat ~/.config/containers/systemd/nfs_n8n.volume 
[Volume]
Device=:/n8n
Driver=local
Options=addr=192.168.20.2,rw,async,hard,intr,nfsvers=4.2
Type=nfs4
[n8n@privatepodman podlet-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu]$ podman volume inspect nfs_n8n
[
     {
          "Name": "nfs_n8n",
          "Driver": "local",
          "Mountpoint": "/srv/n8n/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/nfs_n8n/_data",
          "CreatedAt": "2025-03-21T23:15:39.227804604Z",
          "Labels": {},
          "Scope": "local",
          "Options": {},
          "UID": 1000,
          "GID": 1000,
          "MountCount": 0,
          "NeedsCopyUp": true,
          "NeedsChown": true,
          "LockNumber": 1
     }
]
```
Oh and podman is creating the volume without any issues...
Mar 21 23:15:39 privatepodman podman[7285]: 2025-03-21 23:15:39.235095658 +0000 UTC m=+0.070504764 volume create nfs_n8n


I really am at the end of my knowledge, i really can need some help...
Kind regards
Xyz00777

comments.body:
Looking at your `.volume` file, I can see that you did not set `VolumeName`. As a result, the generated volume name is `systemd-nfs_n8n` and not `nfs_n8n` (the one you call inspect on).

I've also tried the same:
```
$ cat ~/.config/containers/systemd/nfs_n8n.volume
[Volume]
Device=:/n8n
Driver=local
Options=addr=192.168.20.2,rw,async,hard,intr,nfsvers=4.2
Type=nfs4
```
And got:
```
$ podman volume inspect systemd-nfs_n8n
[
     {
          "Name": "systemd-nfs_n8n",
          "Driver": "local",
          "Mountpoint": "/home/yblum.linux/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes/systemd-nfs_n8n/_data",
          "CreatedAt": "2025-03-24T13:32:42.924134499-04:00",
          "Labels": {},
          "Scope": "local",
          "Options": {
               "device": ":/n8n",
               "o": "addr=192.168.20.2,rw,async,hard,intr,nfsvers=4.2",
               "type": "nfs4"
          },
          "MountCount": 0,
          "NeedsCopyUp": true,
          "NeedsChown": true,
          "LockNumber": 9
     }
]
```
It could be that the `nfs_n8n` volume is a leftover from some other tests. In addition, please note that volumes are not deleted when stopping the service and are not recreated when starting. So, you need to manually delete the volume using `podman volume rm` between service restarts when testing.
